summary: Make it possible to run CI manually if `DISABLE_GODOT_CI` is set.

description.text:
There is currently an undocumented feature to disable CI entirely, by setting `DISABLE_GODOT_CI` to `1` in the environment variables.

However, it is impossible right now to manually trigger CI when it is disabled, because if the `Re-run all jobs` button is clicked, it will just skip the CI again.

This PR rectifies this, by ignoring `DISABLE_GODOT_CI` on the second run attempt:

![SCR-20250515-mjlh](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/6b434ff0-cc55-4de2-b3ad-013bba4b8db5)

## Relations
It was proposed in https://github.com/godotengine/godot/pull/75336 to add a manual `workflow_dispatch` trigger. 
However, this trigger would not work for this use case, because if `DISABLE_GODOT_CI` is set, the workflow would be skipped even on manual dispatch.
It would be possible to allow CI to ignore `DISABLE_GODOT_CI` on `workflow_dispatch` events, but as @YuriSizov described in https://github.com/godotengine/godot/pull/75336, there is no reason to add this dispatch if we already have the `Re-run all jobs` button.

summary: Right-click menus open in new windows that you can alt+tab to and they do not auto close when unfocused.

description.text:
### Tested versions

- Reproducible in: v4.5.dev3.official [28089c40c], v4.5.dev3.mono.official [28089c40c], v4.5.dev4.official [209a446e3], v4.5.dev4.mono.official [209a446e3], v4.5.dev.mono.gh [428a762e9], v4.5.dev.mono.gh [45fc515ae], v4.5.dev5.official [64b09905c]
- Not reproducible in: v4.4.1.stable.official [49a5bc7b6], v4.5.dev1.official [97241ffea], v4.5.dev1.mono.official [97241ffea], v4.5.dev2.official [af2c71397], v4.5.dev2.mono.official [af2c71397]

### System information

Linux Mint Cinnamon 22.1 | AMD Ryzen 5500U | Acer Aspire 5 | x11

### Issue description

When you right-click anywhere it spawns a new window which closes automatically only if you right-click in the same origin window (the one you first opened the context menu from). So like if i right-click in the file explorer i can get one menu, but if i right-click in the scene tree and file explorer i can get two!

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/1238dc15-3207-44db-b9ae-e79c605cddf7)

Behavior differs slightly between dev3, dev3 mono, dev4 and dev4 mono, but the point is they all spawn new window frames that at first look like they can be alt-tabbed into, but it's clearly a bug and not supposed to happen because the menus automatically close when you unfocus from the engine or press esc and they have a thing that makes them "appear on top" always.

Very important - i think i hear multiple clicks when i open the bugged engine versions too as i have a sound for when a new window opens. Also i remember somehow managing to cause the bugged engine to have a blank rectangle that i could not close and had to terminate its task from my task manager. Not reproducible, idk how.

When i gave Gemini the full changelog of 4.5-dev3 and the article it pointed me towards https://github.com/godotengine/godot/pull/76829 which makes sense given how big of a change that is, but i have no idea what the change implies so i'm just going to say this is as far as i can direct you. It checks out as a commit made in 4.5-dev3 as this is where the issue starts from and it makes sense that the behavior differs slightly as people are fixing it up constantly. To make sure it hasn't been fixed i did test on v4.5.dev.mono.gh [428a762e9] which is the latest as of writing this.

### Steps to reproduce

Download any of the versions specified from https://godotengine.org/download/archive/, specifically Linux Snadard and .NET. Make a project or open an already existing one. Right-click on different frames (scene tree, file manager, script window).

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

Not project dependent, but i'll be safer than sorry.

summary: [4.5] macOS floating game workspace doesn't receive `InputEventMouseMotion` when `Input.mouse_mode == Input.MOUSE_MODE_CAPTURED`

description.text:
### Tested versions

- Reproducible in: v4.5.dev4
- Not reproducible in: v4.4.1

### System information

Godot v4.5.dev4 - macOS Sequoia (15.4.1) - Multi-window, 1 monitor - Metal (Forward+) - integrated Apple M4 Pro (Apple9) - Apple M4 Pro (14 threads)

### Issue description

When running my game in the floating game workspace, it does not receive the `InputEventMouseMotion` input (from a trackpad on a Macbook Pro) but ONLY when `Input.mouse_mode == Input.MOUSE_MODE_CAPTURED`.

My code is something like this...

```gdscript
func _ready() -> void:
	Input.set_mouse_mode(Input.MOUSE_MODE_CAPTURED)


func _unhandled_input(event: InputEvent):
       print(event, " " , Input.mouse_mode == Input.MOUSE_MODE_CAPTURED)

	if event.is_action_pressed("ui_cancel"):
		if Input.mouse_mode == Input.MOUSE_MODE_CAPTURED:
			Input.set_mouse_mode(Input.MOUSE_MODE_VISIBLE)
		else:
			Input.set_mouse_mode(Input.MOUSE_MODE_CAPTURED)

                get_viewport().set_input_as_handled()

	if event is InputEventMouseMotion and Input.mouse_mode == Input.MOUSE_MODE_CAPTURED:
		_handle_mouse_motion(event) # rotate camera, etc.
                get_viewport().set_input_as_handled()
```

When `Input.mouse_mode == Input.MOUSE_MODE_CAPTURED`, the event is NOT logged at all - almost like it is getting consumed by the viewport somewhere.

When `Input.mouse_mode == Input.MOUSE_MODE_VISIBLE`, for example if I hit Option+Esc to free the mouse, then the event is always logged.

If I un-check "Make Game Workspace Floating on Next Play" so that the game window is embedded in the editor, then the mouse input works as expected without any code changes. This is only specific to the floating game window.

### Steps to reproduce

Try to get `InputEventMouseMotion` inside of `_unhandled_input` when using a floating game window on macOS.

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

I tried to set up an MRP here with this attached to a camera:

```gdscript
extends Node3D


func _ready() -> void:
	Input.set_mouse_mode(Input.MOUSE_MODE_CAPTURED)


func _unhandled_input(event: InputEvent):
	print(event)
	if event.is_action_pressed("ui_cancel"):
		if Input.mouse_mode == Input.MOUSE_MODE_CAPTURED:
			Input.set_mouse_mode(Input.MOUSE_MODE_VISIBLE)
		else:
			Input.set_mouse_mode(Input.MOUSE_MODE_CAPTURED)
		
		get_viewport().set_input_as_handled()

	if event is InputEventMouseMotion and Input.mouse_mode == Input.MOUSE_MODE_CAPTURED:
		rotate_y(-event.relative.x * 0.01)
		
		get_viewport().set_input_as_handled()
```

But in a basic project, it seems to work just fine in both the floating and embedded viewports. I'm not sure what would be different in my setup - I only read this event in one place.

summary: Allow Dock Icon Overrides

description.text:
Related: #91039 

Currently, Editor Docks have two ways to store icons, a StringName (pointing to theme) and a texture2D. The string takes priority and is only assigned upon creation. The icon is able to be assigned through API access (see `EditorPlugin::set_dock_tab_icon`), but it does not take precedence over the StringName, so if you previously set an icon using the StringName then setting it using the icon would not change anything.

Before, there was no method of overriding Dock Icons, and the StringName was rarely used simultaneously with the icon because it was not useful to permanently set a variable that could not then be overridded. This PR changes the order of precedence. This makes it so that the StringName that is assigned can be overridden by the icon.

This is extremely useful if you want a dock to store a default icon, but then want to override it with a temporary icon. For example, when the Output panel is transformed to a dock (see #106426), it would want to override its default icon with the `warning` or `error` icons when issues occur.

summary: Register `DOCK_SLOT_BOTTOM` with `EditorDockManager`

description.text: See: https://github.com/godotengine/godot-proposals/issues/12430 Depends on: #106164 This PR creates a new value in the DockSlot enum, `DOCK_SLOT_BOTTOM`. This change allows the `Move to Bottom` button to be replaced with a button that is more in line with the rest of the docks: This allows the FileSystem dock to move to the bottom (same as master), and notifies it by calling the preexisting `_set_dock_horizontal` method (introduced in https://github.com/godotengine/godot/pull/86765) to allow panels to optionally change to a horizontal layout. This PR temporarily disallows native bottom panels from moving to the other dock panels, which is intended to be amended in future PRs. Additionally, dragging tabs to and from the bottom dock is disallowed because right now we are unable to verify that the drop is correct and notify the docks to change their layout, that is the subject of another PR. There are two commits in this PR. One is a mirror of #106164 that is only included so that this will compile and will be deleted once #106164 is merged. The second is the actual content of this PR. Todo: - [x] Deprecate Bottom Panel methods in EditorPlugin (and the relevant methods in EditorBottomPanel) - [ ] Update to use EditorDock, respect DockLayout - [x] Change EditorSettings Shortcuts to move all the bottom panel shortcuts under dock.

summary: SpringBoneSimulator is missing documentation in the inspector panel

description.text:
### Tested versions

Version 4.4.1 Stable.

### System information

N/A

### Issue description

The new `SpringBoneSimulator3D` is pretty great, but it is missing documentation for all of the properties in the inspector. This means that users of the system either have to read the engine code or go chase down the docs when trying to remember what each of the settings does. Unfortunately, the online docs also do not appear to describe what the various properties in the inspector do (https://docs.godotengine.org/en/latest/classes/class_springbonesimulator3d.html)

It would be great if documentation could be added, as this would reduce the number of misunderstandings and support questions on forums associated with using this new node.

For example, by default SpringBoneSimulator3D configures the "Gravity" value as "0.0" which appears to disable the animations. It would be good to know that adjusting this value is required.

Other examples of questions that could be answered by having the tooltips for inspector properties filled out:

- "does WorldOrigin mean the current scene world origin or the top level world origin the scene is instantiated in?"
- "what does Extend End Bone due and why would one need to use it?"
- "what do the damping curves curve along?"

This node has great potential, but that potential is currently only available to people who can read and understand the Engine source.

### Steps to reproduce

Hover over any property of SpringBoneSimulator3D in the inspector, or try to find information about the properties of this node in online documentation (https://docs.godotengine.org/en/latest/classes/class_springbonesimulator3d.html)

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

N/A

summary: Channel Remap: Information not preserved when remapping to a non-existent destination channel

description.text:
### Tested versions

- Reproducible in: v4.5.dev.mono.gh [428a762e9]

### System information

Godot v4.5.dev.mono (428a762e9) - Linux Mint 22.1 (Xia) on X11 - X11 display driver, Multi-window, 1 monitor - Vulkan (Forward+) - dedicated NVIDIA GeForce GTX 960 (nvidia; 550.144.03) - AMD FX(tm)-6300 Six-Core Processor (6 threads)

### Issue description

Recently, the feature [Channel Remap](https://github.com/godotengine/godot/pull/99676) was added, but it has a behavior that can be considered problematic when trying to remap a channel to a non-existent channel in the texture.

![Unity](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/0d3124b5-f7ef-4c7d-b6fb-02b4be273203)
As you can see, Unity allowed me to move the red channel to the alpha channel.

![Godot](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/25c06933-4958-48c2-9595-7c4840b1f139)
In the case of Godot, it does create the alpha channel but leaves it blank.

### Steps to reproduce

1. Select an RGB texture.
2. Go to the import dock.
3. Remap the Red channel to Zero.
4. Remap the Blue channel to Zero.
5. Remap the Alpha channel to Red.
6. Reimport the texture.

summary: Writing an .exr texture and then trying to immediately load it with ResourceLoader fails in a tool script

description.text:
### Tested versions

Godot v4.5.dev3 - Windows 10 (build 19045) - Multi-window, 2 monitors - Vulkan (Forward+) - dedicated NVIDIA GeForce RTX 3090 (NVIDIA; 32.0.15.6094) - AMD Ryzen 9 3950X 16-Core Processor (32 threads)

### System information

Godot v4.5.dev3 - Windows 10 (build 19045) - Multi-window, 2 monitors - Vulkan (Forward+) - dedicated NVIDIA GeForce RTX 3090 (NVIDIA; 32.0.15.6094) - AMD Ryzen 9 3950X 16-Core Processor (32 threads)

### Issue description

Issue Summary:

I have a small tool script, which generates a .exr file for use with shaders/particle systems.
I can make my .exr file and see it appear in the FileSystem explorer, but I am unable to ResourceLoader.load() that texture after immediately writing it out successfully. I can check for the existence of the file, and even open it with the FileAccess singleton. But ResourceLoader doesnt seem to be able to immediately 'see its existence' and I have failed to find documentation that explains why this is the case.
If I run the toolscript function a second time, then suddenly ResourceLoader is able to see the file and other code functions correctly.

The editor's relationship with saving files, being able to open them as a file, or a resource, and trying to assign a resource to an object/inspector field gives unintuitive results.

More detailed explanation :
-For the MRP i've simplified the generation and output of the tool script code to make a randomly colored 16x16 pixel .exr file.
- I can successfully generate the .exr file, but trying to load the generated .exr file immediately after writing it, fails when using ResourceLoader.load() its not very clear as to why this fails, since i'm not calling a threaded load , and assumed that this would be a blocking operation until the load has completed, or the resource loader would be able to load the file if FileAccess can see+open the file beforehand after the .exr file was successfully written.
- The intention of me using  ResourceLoader.load() is so I can have the tool auto-assign script generated textures to the inspector as an exportable Texture2D, which I would also like to have assigned to materials, shaders, etc.

example code snippet of the code file make_emission_tex.gd
```
@export var baked_data:Texture2D = null # where the .exr file generated by the tool script should be assigned

....
	# assign the texture to the inspector
	# baked_data
	baked_data = ResourceLoader.load( path_exr, "", ResourceLoader.CACHE_MODE_IGNORE )

*and*

	if baked_data :
		process_material.emission_point_texture = baked_data

```
- If you press the toolscript 'bake_exr' button **twice** youll see the generated .exr assigned in 2 locations in the inspector panel for the GPUParticles3D object that has the attached script make_emission_tex.gd

- Below is the output of the script when pressing the 'bake_exr' tool button **ONCE** in make_emission_tex
The test_emission_tex.exr is successfully created, and verified to exist by immediately calling this code section
```
	# check if it exists first.
	var doesFileExist:bool = FileAccess.file_exists(path_exr)
	print(" doesFileExist ", path_exr, " : ", doesFileExist )
	
	var file = FileAccess.open(path_exr, FileAccess.READ)
	print("file ", file)
	if file:
		var content = file.get_as_text()
		print("content as text : ", content )
		file.flush()
		file.close()
	else:
		print("failed to open file ", path_exr )

```
using 'FileAccess' I can verify the existence of the file in the script, as well as open it, and calling
EditorInterface.get_resource_filesystem() and scan() will refresh the FileSystem to show the newly generated .EXR
![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/5f8ffc11-8e6e-4ba3-a3cc-ca063071bcbb)


**HOWEVER**
Trying to use the ResourceLoader.load() with the optional cache_mode parameter or not, **FAILS** to load the resource.
Pressing the toolmode button 'bake_exr' a **SECOND TIME** to regenerate the .exr file **again** the ResourceLoader is able to load the .exr file of the same name.
Here is the output log after pressing the button a second time:

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/330efa06-5e9e-45d4-9539-48325b01ae2d)
and since ResourceLoader was successful at loading the .exr file, I can now assign a reference to it in multiple spots, such as the particle process material
Now I get the expected result after writing over the existing .exr with a second run of the tool script.
![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/c179e56b-818b-4369-b711-3f147550fe2e)

Related to another reported bug : if you click and drag the assigned texture even the slightest when opening the inspector, you will full crash the editor 100%


The image below is an example of successful .exr file generation and loading of the resource with this MRP after doing the 2 button presses, as well as assignment to 2 locations in the inspector.
The particle system is now using the random position data in the .exr file to determine their spawning position.
As mentioned before, these results only happen if you press the 'bake_exr' button **TWICE**
I would like to know what's missing in the implementation of the example tool script, so I only need to press it once instead of twice.
I dont know if this is a bug, or a limitation of the editor, or I am missing some information, or a combination of things. thank you :)

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/4c83b5c8-d8fa-4fba-8da2-9f77f34eb385)


### Steps to reproduce

Open the test_scene.tscn

- Go to the 3D view and Select the GPUParticles3D node
- Look at the inspector panel for the particle system, and click the 'bake_exr' button
- Observe the generated texture in the FileSystem, but it not being assigned in any inspector panel locations.
- Look at the Output Log

summary: GPUParticles3D Positionally Stutter in different ways depending on editor version when using lowered speed scale, with default / varying Fixed Fps settings

description.text:
### Tested versions



Godot v4.3.stable - Windows 10.0.19045 - Vulkan (Forward+) - dedicated NVIDIA GeForce RTX 3090 (NVIDIA; 32.0.15.6094) - AMD Ryzen 9 3950X 16-Core Processor (32 Threads)

Godot v4.4.1.stable - Windows 10 (build 19045) - Multi-window, 2 monitors - Vulkan (Forward+) - dedicated NVIDIA GeForce RTX 3090 (NVIDIA; 32.0.15.6094) - AMD Ryzen 9 3950X 16-Core Processor (32 threads)

Godot v4.5.dev3 - Windows 10 (build 19045) - Multi-window, 2 monitors - Vulkan (Forward+) - dedicated NVIDIA GeForce RTX 3090 (NVIDIA; 32.0.15.6094) - AMD Ryzen 9 3950X 16-Core Processor (32 threads)


### System information

Godot v4.5.dev3 - Windows 10 (build 19045) - Multi-window, 2 monitors - Vulkan (Forward+) - dedicated NVIDIA GeForce RTX 3090 (NVIDIA; 32.0.15.6094) - AMD Ryzen 9 3950X 16-Core Processor (32 threads)

### Issue description

The default FixFps sim time (30fps) of GpuParticles3D with a lower speed scale settings (less than 1.0), while having **Interpolate** **and** **FractDelta** creates very noticable positional stutter that interpolate and then jump.
This visual behavior seems noticably different than 4.3stable versus newer versions of the editor.
The expected behavior is that an interpolated shader would more evenly have a positional jumping if any. The videos below can illustrate the strange interpolation behavior.

Vsync is set to ON for both the project *and* the editor.
Of note, the FPS reported in frame time seems to be extremely high and not representative of the actual rendering occurring in the editor viewport as well.

The only workaround is to not use the default setting and increase the setting to 60/120 or even 240fps depending on the vfx content. I have started to just use 60 or in most cases 120fps fixedfps setting to get visually smooth particle sims, especially ones that are slower moving or for intentionally 'slow-mo' aesthetic use cases.

The current default particle settings with normal timescale of 1.0 and earth gravity hide a lot of the stuttering, but there is a lot of particle use cases that don't use these settings and the stuttering is very noticeable, which is why I have to raise the setting on all particle systems I am making.

Any sort of 'time stop' or 'time dilation' particle effect desired by adjusting the speed scale parameters in advance, or dynamically will experience this stuttering and has to run at a FixedFps setting of 120+ to hide these artifacts.

As a side comment, i'm not sure if the current 30fps default setting is a good default for any project targeting devices other than lower end mobile devices.

### Steps to reproduce

- create a particle system
- assign a particle process material + mesh object to represent the particles (builtin box was used in the example)
- adjust the 'speed scale' in the Time category of a GPUParticles3D to a value lower than 1, values as they approach 0.0 will start to display stuttering if the FixedFps is not set to an extremely high value instead of the default.
- observe the default stuttering with FixedFps of 30, and Interpolate+FractDelta turned ON
- increase the setting to 60, 120, 240 to see the diffrences.
- VSYNC is ON for both Editor and Project settings
- The visual result is the same for both running the MRP, as well as viewing it in an editor viewport or thru a camera preview.
- setting speed scale value to 0.01 you can see positional interpolation, and then erratic 'jumps' in positioning every few seconds.

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/2bea5d20-4076-4056-9fec-115f4107943b



I am testing and reporting this on 3 different editor versions since I have a project that mostly exists in 4.3stable, but there are other issues in 4.4.1 and 4.5 dev that are preventing me from committing to fully migrating my work off of 4.3stable.
A lot of my assets are particle system related, and i've been working around them by increasing the FixedFps setting to 120 or 240, but I don't know if this is a safe workaround moving forward and if there is a bug here in the engine.
I also write my own particle processing shaders in addition to using the default ones, so some advice on best practices here with using FixedFps is appreciated.



This is the stuttering movement in godot 4.3, which is noticably different than the stuttering movement in 4.4.1 and 4.5dev3

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/44794554-31cc-4f38-ac5b-8b14c8940e8f)

I would expect the fields to interpret the expression, like the inspector fields would. Or at least zero out the fields instead of keeping the invalid values ("a" for rotation snap) there.

[Link to the Snap Settings in source code](https://github.com/godotengine/godot/blob/09fcbb86459498985a910cdd1684844a1968dc96/editor/plugins/node_3d_editor_plugin.h#L778), they are all plain LineEdits.

Also the invalid values (like the letter `a`) stay in the dialog fields when it's closed and opened again. I'd expect the fields it to reflect the _interpreted_ values - if not now, then surely after closing and opening.

summary: Use notification Ready instead of EnterTree when connecting Signals

description.text:
This PR shares the same overarching goal as #106164. The Bottom Panel should be more integrated into the dock system, and this PR builds towards that.

Currently, the theme editor, animation, and debugger panels (that you can access from the bottom panel) connect signals that never change in their ENTER_TREE notifications. Normally this is not an issue because these panels do not repeatedly enter and exit the tree, however if these panels move between different parents (say, if you wanted to move the animation panel to the right dock, it would leave the tree then re-enter) this would print errors as it tries to connect signals that are already connected.

In essence, signals should only be connected in `NOTIFICATION_ENTER_TREE` if they are also disconnected in `NOTIFICATION_EXIT_TREE`. This PR changes `NOTIFICATION_ENTER_TREE` to `NOTIFICATION_READY`. This makes it so that the signals are only ever connected once. Even if these specific panels are not made moveable, this bug still should be corrected.

The `NOTIFICATION_ENTER_TREE` can be turned into a `NOTIFICATION_READY` if the class owns the objects that are connecting to it, otherwise they should be disconnected in `NOTIFICATION_EXIT_TREE`.

Instead of reworking how the grid map editor functions (which probably should be done in another PR), this PR simply disconnects the offending signal in `NOTIFICATION_EXIT_TREE`.

summary: macOS: Send initial modifier keys as input events

description.text:
A follow-on to #106317.

Ensure modifier key events are pushed to the `Input` system when Godot is activated. This is important for actions that read modifier keys to alter their own behaviour. An example is clicking the zoom in / out buttons to force integer zoom:

https://github.com/godotengine/godot/blob/d32d2e9b511dbdce4e79621a92c63088c9912ad0/editor/gui/editor_zoom_widget.cpp#L54

An example of this working, where Godot is not focused, but correctly handles the integer zoom:

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/5cdc7e42-118a-40cb-b6cf-cd9f40cae3e3


> [!NOTE]
>
> The mouse click event does have the correct modifier state, but Godot reads the keyboard pressed state, and therefore we push these special set of keys. A more reliable approach would be to raise two separate events, or allow the `pressed` event to optionally include the source event,

summary: Editor crash from AnimationPlayer under certain circumstances

description.text:
### Tested versions

v4.4.1.stable.mono.official [49a5bc7b6]

### System information

Godot v4.4.1.stable.mono - Windows 10 (build 19045) - Multi-window, 2 monitors - OpenGL 3 (Compatibility) - NVIDIA GeForce RTX 2070 (NVIDIA; 32.0.15.6094) - 12th Gen Intel(R) Core(TM) i7-12700KF (20 threads)

### Issue description

Calling an AnimationPlayer's play method via signals or a tool script, while in the editor, can sometimes crash the editor. From my testing, these are the conditions for the crash:
* The animation player dock is open
* The animation being called to play is not the same as the one already playing
* Any node of any type other than AnimationPlayer is selected in the scene tree
* No tracks or keyframes need to exist in the AnimationPlayer - this crash happens regardless of the contents of the animations.

There may be another condition related to the animation player having entered/exited the tree at some point (i.e. switching editor tabs) but I haven't been able to pinpoint exactly what it is.

### Steps to reproduce

1. Open the attached project.
2. Open the two scenes included. (blank and crash_test)
3. In crash_test, select the AnimationPlayer node and change the animation to RESET.
4. Select a different node in the scene, whilst keeping the animation dock open.
5. Switch to the "blank" tab
6. Switch back to the "crash_test" tab
7. After 1 second, the editor crashes.

For context, the setup for the crash_test scene is this:
* The root node's enter_tree signal gets fired in the editor when switching to the crash_test tab. This is used to execute code in the editor and trigger the bug (a tool script also works here)
* That signal starts a 1 second timer (used to prove that the bug isn't caused by calling methods during a sensitive code path such as tree-related notifications)
* After the timer has elapsed, it calls the AnimationPlayer's play method.

No relevant errors seem to appear in the logs (when opening the editor via the console with the --verbose flag) - though a single time during testing on my own project, I got an AccessViolationException from the EmitSignal method, when the signal was emitted via a tool script. This may or may not be related.

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/17650c0b-3a89-473f-a643-ca062e37fc81

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

[animation-player-crash-test.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/20193393/animation-player-crash-test.zip)

### Issue description

Building a keyboard controllable interface with a mixture of buttons and LineEdits. When I use arrow keys to get focus on a text field, I can't type in the field. Using the mouse to click on the text field works of course, but that's not fully in the spirit of the keyboard interface. The tab key can actually get focus on text fields that allows for typing, but that involves making the user break away from the arrow key paradigm used for the rest of menu navigation, which is a bit undesired. Typing focus can also be gained by pressing enter, then beginning to type, but it's not immediately intuitive. This might be classifiable as a proposal, though I do strongly feel like the current behavior is quite undesirable.

summary: Increase float precision in the editor inspector for Quaternions

description.text:
Made in response to #106335. For Quaternions, it's better to display more digits in the inspector for precision, beyond the usual float step found in the editor settings in `"interface/inspector/default_float_step"`. Note that property hints can still override the step to a different value, this change only overrides the editor setting.

This PR also adjusts the `EditorSpinSlider` code to clamp the step used for dragging to a minimum of 0.001. Without this change, clicking and dragging on Quaternion elements becomes very slow. I didn't even test double precision, but imagine how slow that would be too. This may affect editor plugins that expect this behavior, but I don't think it's good behavior. It's bad to discourage users from using high precision because the usability is worse. Perhaps there needs to be separate settings for drag step and the actual number step, but that's a larger proposal...

_Bugsquad Edited:_
- Fixes https://github.com/godotengine/godot/issues/98090

summary: Implement automatic snapping in split containers

description.text:
Closes https://github.com/godotengine/godot-proposals/issues/12417.

Implement automatic snapping in split containers so that when the user drags the splitter beyond a threshold after the minimum size of a child, it "snaps" to the appropriate edge (left/right for horizontal arrangement, top/bottom for vertical arrangement), causing the child look as if it disappeared. The splitter can still be dragged back to reveal the child.

Visually this looks similar to collapsing but functionally it differs as it only affects the visual state of the nodes.

The proposal contains a video of this in action.

- *Production edit: This partially addresses
https://github.com/godotengine/godot-proposals/issues/1441 (requires mouse to hide individual docks).*

summary: macOS: Process first click event without requiring focus

description.text:
This improves the user experience for macOS users, to be consistent with other macOS applications. When a Godot window is not focused, and the user clicks on the window, the event is forwarded to the receiving control to be processed as if the window was already focused. This is the same behaviour as applications like Safari, Blender, CLion, Xcode, etc.

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/fe9f708e-0f1a-4260-9f9a-87ddd982eaec

> [!NOTE]
>
> When the window focus is on an application other than the Godot editor, and the traffic lights are greyed out, I can still click **Stop**

Next, I launch with a separate embedded window

> [!NOTE]
>
> When the separate embedded window is focused, I can still click **Stop**

description.text:

I also tested this on Godot 4.3.stable and observed the same issue, so it does not appear to be a recent regression introduced in 4.4.

2.  Create a minimal scene with a Node2D and Sprite2D

description.text:
### Tested versions

- Reproducable on Godot v4.4.1.stable.arch_linux

Godot v4.4.1.stable unknown - Arch Linux (KDE Plasma Wayland as DE) #1 SMP PREEMPT_DYNAMIC Fri, 09 May 2025 17:20:29 +0000 on Wayland - Wayland display driver, Single-window, 1 monitor - Vulkan (Mobile) - integrated AMD Radeon Graphics (RADV RENOIR) - AMD Ryzen 5 5600H with Radeon Graphics (12 threads)

### Issue description

I have Emulate Touch From Mouse turned on and use the following function to get start and end point of a drag:
```gdscript
var startpos = null
var endpos = null
func _input(event: InputEvent) -> void:
	if event is InputEventScreenTouch:
		if event.is_pressed():
			startpos = event.get_position()
		if event.is_released():
			endpos = event.get_position()
			_launch(startpos, endpos)
```
When end of drag is inside the game window, it passes it to _launch in no time, but when it's outside of the window - it takes a second or so. This issue is not present on X11 or Windows builds of the game run under Wine in Wayland native mode

### Steps to reproduce

- Enable Wayland in project settings
- Enable Emulate Touch From Mouse
- Use the code below in a node of a scene
- Click and hold LMB in the game window, move it out of the window and release it
```gdscript
func _input(event: InputEvent) -> void:
	if event is InputEventScreenTouch:
		if event.is_released():
			print("End of drag")
```

description.text:
### Tested versions

v4.4.1.stable.mono.official [49a5bc7b6]

### System information

Godot v4.4.1.stable.mono - Windows 11 (build 22631) - Multi-window, 1 monitor - OpenGL 3 (Compatibility) - NVIDIA GeForce RTX 3050 Laptop GPU (NVIDIA; 32.0.15.6070) - 11th Gen Intel(R) Core(TM) i7-11800H @ 2.30GHz (16 threads)

### Issue description

If an instantiable scene has a MultiplayerSynchronizer node, all other MultiplayerSynchronizer nodes in that scene are modified when you turn on editable children to modify synchronization properties.

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/129d20fe-08ba-4a2e-95f9-5603f2677a02

### Steps to reproduce

1. Create a scene (Scene 1), using Node2D as the root node.
2. Create a MultiplayerSynchronizer child node in the scene.

summary: Window `canvas_item_default_texture_filter` does not work as expected

description.text:
### Tested versions

- Reproducible in Godot-v4.4.1-stable
- Reproducible in Godot-v4.5-dev3

### System information

Godot v4.4.1.stable - Windows 10 (build 19045) - Multi-window, 2 monitors - Vulkan (Forward+) - dedicated NVIDIA GeForce GTX 970 (NVIDIA; 32.0.15.7247) - AMD Ryzen 7 7800X3D 8-Core Processor (16 threads)

### Issue description

The `Viewport`-node has a `canvas_item_default_texture_filter` which is to be used to define the filtering behaviour of any of its children. This property does not have any effect at all.

# Observed behaviour 
![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/4c900bb5-42de-4e37-a3c8-d9e391045c88)

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/90308da6-ac1f-4bf3-a4b9-10cfed73a2e4)

Explanation:
- The upper label just uses the `canvas_item_default_texture_filter`-property which is set in the project settings to LINEAR -> we expect the text to be filtered => SUCCESS
- The left window has its `canvas_item_default_texture_filter`-property set to LINEAR -> we expect the text to be filtered => SUCCESS
- The left window has its `canvas_item_default_texture_filter`-property set to NEAREST -> we expect the text to have no filtering => FAIL

Things I tried:
- Change the filtering property of each label from INHERITED to their respective wanted filtering value:
  - Upper label listens correctly to the filtering property => SUCCESS
  - The left window's label seemingly ignores its own filtering property => FAIL
  - The right window's label seemingly ignores its own filtering property => FAIL

When I set the `canvas_item_default_texture_filter`-property of the project settings to NEAREST I get the inverse issue (where everything is now NEAREST and I can't get the viewport to be filtered...)

# Expected behaviour

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/7e7565f7-5f0d-4399-8cd2-2e25d6473446)

### Steps to reproduce

1. Create a new Control node
2. Add two Window node children
3. Add a Label node to each of the Windows
4. Write the exact same text in both of the Labels
5. Set the `canvas_item_default_texture_filter`-property of the first Window to LINEAR
6. Set the `canvas_item_default_texture_filter`-property of the second Window to NEAREST
7. Look at the text of the Labels and observe that both of them are filtered even though one of them is set NEAREST.

summary: Use a fragment shader copy instead of a blit copy in the final blit to screen in the Compatibility backend

description.text:
Fixes: https://github.com/godotengine/godot/issues/102352
Fixes: https://github.com/godotengine/godot/issues/18320

When Android users go into the developer options and enable "Force 4x MSAA", Android forces us to allocate a 4X MSAA backbuffer. It happens automatically and we can't stop it, despite it being a complete waste of resources. 

Unfortunately the common advice on the internet for Android gamers is that they _should always_ enable "Force 4x MSAA" (for example, this horrifying Quora post https://www.quora.com/Is-it-safe-to-turn-on-the-Force-4x-MSAA-on-Android?top_ans=105185416). So many Android gamers are activating developer mode (despite the numerous warnings that Android gives saying not to) and turning on the setting. 

Currently we blit our internal framebuffer to the main backbuffer. However, `glBlitFramebuffer` only works if you are blitting from a multisampled texture into a non-multisampled texture, or if the number of samples is the same between the two. By enabling "Force 4x MSAA" we can end up blitting from a non MSAA texture into a 4X MSAA texture, which fails. 

Fortunately, the workaround is very easy and not harmful for performance. We just use a fragment shader to copy over the texture. This has the benefit of working for any sample count and isn't more expensive than blitting. 

CC @dsnopek The only part I am unsure about is the texture copy for Multiview. I believe what I have should work fine for WebXR, but can you check and make sure?

summary: Filtered scene tree rearranges/hides results erroneously when a tool script spawns/deletes nodes

description.text:
### Tested versions

Reproducible in `4.4.1.stable`, `4.5.dev3`
**NOT** reproducible in `4.3.stable`

### System information

Godot v4.4.1.stable - Windows 11 (build 22631) - Multi-window, 1 monitor - Vulkan (Forward+) - dedicated NVIDIA GeForce GTX 1660 SUPER (NVIDIA; 32.0.15.6636) - Intel(R) Core(TM) i7-10700F CPU @ 2.90GHz (16 threads)

### Issue description

When you type something in the filter bar of the scene tree, and you have a tool script that adds/removes nodes in the scene, sometimes it will cause the scene tree to erroneously rearrange its contents. The nature of this rearrangement ranges from items disappearing/reappearing to items moving back and forth between two different locations, or some combination of the two. Sometimes it will happen one time, and sometimes it will keep repeating.

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/8835ee24-d9ae-4bb4-bebf-2058595cd1b2)
![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/75111258-539a-4892-aba3-b05cd7e90177)

I have tool scripts for spawning effects like clouds in the sky, or wind trails. These nodes are added to the scene, but they're not owned by the scene, so they aren't saved, and they do not even show up in the scene tree. The fact that they are causing the filtered view of scene nodes to change is strange, but also very disruptive. Trying to select things while having a filter active is sometimes like a game of cat-and-mouse.

**EDIT:** The issue seems to only happen if the `Hide Filtered Out Parents` option is enabled.

### Steps to reproduce

I do not have a reliable repro yet, but all I know is that the problem goes away if I get rid of the spawning routines in the tool scripts. I could not repro this on a minimal project.

N/A
